iterations.strategy: Category B (physical implementation mismatch): The iter_0 inner loop used long double (80-bit x87 FPU) for accumulation, which prevents SSE/AVX vectorization and is 3-5x slower per operation than double. With ~6.5M qualifying rows in the hot loop, this is the dominant cost. Fix: (1) Replace all long double with double throughout — aggregation arrays, merge, threshold computation. This enables auto-vectorization of the inner accumulation loop. (2) Use madvise(MADV_SEQUENTIAL) on the scanned range of sub_fk.bin and value.bin instead of MAP_POPULATE on entire files — reduces page fault overhead. (3) Parallelize the merge phase: instead of single-threaded iteration over 64×10K entries, each thread reduces a slice of group_ids. (4) Increase morsel size to 128K for better instruction cache utilization and reduced scheduling overhead. (5) Use a separate uint8_t bitmap for fy2022 qualification check (86KB, fits L2) for branch-free pre-filtering before group_id lookup. Format output with %.2f for double.
